responseMessageContents.text:
The user just said "Hi". But there's a system context saying this is a NEW empty project and I should read the platforms skill and create an app. However, the prior conversation context shows a massive existing project with hundreds of files, git commits, backend, expo app, iOS app, etc.

The system_context says "This is a NEW empty project" but the file listing shows a fully populated workspace with backend/, expo/, ios-ivx/, etc. This seems contradictory.

Let me look more carefully. The system context says it's a new empty project and my first action must be to read the platforms skill. But the file listing clearly shows an existing project with hundreds of files. The prior conversation summary also confirms extensive work on an existing IVX Holdings project.

The user just said "Hi" - this is a simple greeting. I should respond conversationally and ask what they'd like to do. I shouldn't jump into creating a new app when there's clearly an existing project here, and the user just said "Hi".

The system_context instruction says to use the context to continue/finish the task but never mention it. The new_project context seems like it might be a stale/template context that doesn't match the actual workspace state.

Given the existing project with hundreds of files, I should respond naturally to the "Hi" greeting and ask how I can help.

Hi! I see you have an existing IVX Holdings project here — Expo app, backend, iOS app, and landing page, all live at ivxholding.com.

What would you like to work on?
